data.title: Oil Prices Jump after Iran Claims Control of Strait of Hormuz — 3 Top Energy Stocks to Watch Now
data.description:
Oil prices moved higher after Iran's Revolutionary Guard said it has taken control of the Strait of Hormuz and warned ships not to pass through the route. The development raised new concerns about global oil supply and increased fears of a wider conflict in the Middle East.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Following the development, Brent crude prices extended a four-day rally, rising 2.65% to $83.60 per barrel on Wednesday, hovering near the highest level since January 2025. U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude also climbed for a third straight session to $76.45. The rise in prices is putting major oil producers such as Devon Energy (DVN), SLB (SLB) and Diamondback Energy (FANG) in focus.

Why the Strait of Hormuz Matters

Iran lies next to the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway that carries a large share of the world's oil shipments. The route handles a significant portion of global crude exports, making it one of the most important chokepoints in the energy market.

Because so much oil passes through this corridor, any threat to tanker traffic can quickly push prices higher. Analysts said that if tanker traffic remains limited, oil prices could stay elevated in the near term. As a result, we identified two "Strong Buy" energy companies using the TipRanks database that could benefit from the rise in oil prices.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: VOO vs. VTI: Which Vanguard ETF Is the Smarter Buy for 2026?
data.description:
Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) and Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI) are both core Vanguard ETFs, but they aren't exactly the same. VOO tracks the S&P 500 and focuses only on large U.S. companies. VTI, on the other hand, invests in the entire U.S. stock market. Using the TipRanks' ETF Comparison Tool, we have placed VOO and VTI against each other to find the best Vanguard ETF for investors in 2026.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/4/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Rebound Despite Escalating Iran Conflict
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rebounded early Wednesday despite escalating tensions from the U.S.-Iran conflict. Oil prices surged after reports that Iran's Revolutionary Guard seized control of the Strait of Hormuz, warning ships to stay clear and halting tanker traffic through this key global oil corridor.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.30%, 0.22%, and 0.10%, respectively, at 5:31 a.m. EST on March 4.

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed lower after early-session plunges marked some of their worst declines in years. The S&P 500 closed down 0.94%, the tech-heavy Nasdaq dropped 1.02%, and the Dow fell 0.83%.

President Trump stated last night that the U.S. Navy stands ready to escort tankers through the Strait of Hormuz despite Iran's actions. Nonetheless, oil prices continued rising on supply fears, with Brent crude up 2.55% at $83.95 per barrel, near its highest since January 2025.

Turning to the economic calendar, data on ADP private payrolls will be released today.

On the earnings front, notable companies reporting today include Broadcom (AVGO), Rigetti Computing (RGTI), ChargePoint (CHPT), Okta (OKTA), and Beyond Meat (BYND).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.08%. WTI crude oil futures shot up to about $76.60 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price traded around $5,195 per ounce on Wednesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today amid a slew of corporate earnings releases and Middle East tensions.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Lower

Asia-Pacific markets fell on March 4 as the Iran conflict weighed on sentiment.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index declined 2.01%. In China, the Shanghai Composite fell 0.98%, while the Shenzhen Component dropped 0.71%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei shed 3.61%, with the Topix down 3.67%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Do Not Miss These 3 Stock Picks, Says Legendary Analyst
data.description:
Today, I'd like to focus on three picks from TheStreet Pro's awesome analyst Stephen "Sarge" Guilfoyle. I talk a lot about Sarge here, not only because he has great picks, but also because he owns his mistakes. And those are the two qualities that make traders legendary.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Did I just say Sarge is a legend? Yes. I did. As we've discussed here before, Sarge's top annual picks for TheStreet Pro have earned triple-digit returns. He told readers to buy AMD (AMD) in 2023, which then shot up 128%. Then, he became a fan of Palantir stock (PLTR), which jumped 340% in 2024 before tacking on 135% in 2025. So, yeah, legend.

Join us on TheStreet Pro and never miss another move.

This year, he picked SoFi (SOFI), and it hasn't worked out so well, falling around 30%. We've covered Sarge's rationale for buying SOFI stock, and how he's dealing with the decline. But now, Sarge is seeing reasons to be optimistic about SoFi. And while nobody loves war, Sarge has done his time in national service and has two stock picks that could surge following the U.S. attack on Iran.

A $29 Price Target for SOFI

For the record, I'm not only covering SoFi because Sarge loves the company. I'm including it here because the trade hasn't worked out yet, and it's important for people to understand how a great trader attempts to turn a loss into a winner.

If you recall, Sarge is still long shares from a very low price. As the stock rolled over, he added to his position, but later sold those shares for a loss when the risk became too high. Last week, he began to increase his position for the following reasons:

  1. CEO Anthony Noto signaled his own confidence in the company, buying $1 million worth of shares.
  2. A partnership with Mastercard (MA) allows for SOFI's U.S. Dollar Stablecoin to be settled on MA's multi-token network. 
  3. The price chart looks supportive. While the 50% retracement of the rally from August 2024 through November 2025 held briefly, it's the 61.8% retracement that offers better support at $16.

So, between the news of the CEO buying shares, the partnership with Mastercard, and the stock trading down to support, Sarge is willing to give this one another shot. His first target is the 200-day SMA at $23, but he's still looking for $29 in 2026, more than 50% above Tuesday's last sale.

You can read more at "Sticking with this SoFi target after major insider buying, Mastercard partnership."

A Wartime Market Winner with a New Price Target

It seems as though more and more defense strategy takes place away from the battlefield. Drones are a big part of this. Sarge's pick, Ondas Holdings (ONDS), is a company that makes drones, among other things. TipRanks tells us that they operate in two segments, Ondas Networks and American Robotics. The Waltham, Massachusetts-based company sells to users in rail, energy, agriculture, and critical infrastructure worldwide.

On Monday, the company announced a $10 million investment in World View Enterprises that includes a collaboration to develop intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance solutions for commercial and defense-related clientele, including the U.S. Department of War and the Department of Homeland Security.

It's not just Sarge who's a fan. TipRanks tells us that the eight analysts who cover the stock have a price target that's about 75% above current levels, although Sarge says that their recent actions indicate a lack of consensus on the upcoming earnings; two have revised higher, two lower, and four are holding still.
data.author: Jason Meshnick, The Street Pro

data.title: Ocado Group Stock Plunges Nearly 20% as Tech Revenue Outlook Misses Wall Street
data.description:
Shares of Ocado Group (OCDGF) fell nearly 20% in early trading on March 4 after the firm gave a weak outlook for next year. The drop came even though the U.K. tech and food firm posted a fair set of full-year results. However, the market chose to focus on the year ahead.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ocado said its 2025 sales rose 12% to about $1.8 billion, with gains in its tech unit that sells robot-run food hubs to grocers. The firm also said profit on an EBITDA basis rose from last year. Still, the tone of its 2026 view drew the most heat from the market.

For the next year, the firm said tech unit sales may reach about $640 million. That is far below the roughly $775 million that many on the Street had in mind. In the same note, the firm said EBITDA margin may be near 30%, while many had looked for about 33%.

In its own words, Ocado said the next year will be a time of "steady growth with cost focus." Yet the gap with Wall Street views led many to cut their near-term hopes for the firm.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Is It Time to Buy the Dip in Micron Stock (MU) after the Recent Plunge?
data.description:
Micron (MU) shares slipped 8% on Tuesday as broader markets reacted to rising geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran. The recent decline also reflects renewed worries about increasing HBM competition and the risk of cyclical oversupply in the memory chip sector. Over the past five days, MU stock has fallen about 11%. Looking ahead, analysts remain bullish on Micron, though potential upside may be limited after last year's significant share price surge.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Micron designs and manufactures memory and storage solutions, including DRAM and NAND chips, for computers, smartphones, and data centers.

What Lies Ahead for Micron?

Micron is set to report its Q2 FY26 results on March 18, with Wall Street forecasting earnings of $8.52 per share on revenue of $18.85 billion. Investors will be closely watching the report for guidance on demand trends, pricing, and supply dynamics in the memory chip market.

Micron faces a mix of opportunities and risks as it heads into 2026. On the positive side, strong demand for AI, cloud computing, and data center memory could support long-term growth. Analysts remain bullish, expecting solid earnings in the upcoming Q2 FY26 report.

On Wall Street, Stifel Nicolaus' five-star-rated analyst Brian Chin has the highest price target of $550 on MU stock, implying an upside of over 30%. Chin noted that memory prices are hitting higher levels, adding that the real opportunity lies not just in HBM for AI chips, but also in server DDR5. With the gap between supply and demand widening, Stifel believes the Wall Street consensus is too low, underestimating the potential for upward earnings revisions in the coming quarters.

Key Risks for Micron

On the flip side, one key challenge for Micron and other memory makers is capacity constraints. Factories can't ramp up supply quickly due to limited space, long equipment lead times, and a shortage of skilled workers. Most of the new production is being directed toward high-bandwidth memory (HBM) for AI chips, leaving DRAM and NAND markets tight, which could keep prices elevated in the near term.

For context, DRAM (Dynamic Random Access Memory) is high-speed memory used for temporary data storage in devices like computers, servers, and smartphones. In contrast, NAND is a type of flash memory used for long-term storage, commonly found in SSDs and mobile devices to retain data even when powered off.

Additionally, the stock has already surged over the past year, limiting near-term upside. Investors also face risks from cyclical oversupply, rising HBM competition, and broader market volatility driven by geopolitical tensions.

Overall, MU may offer long-term growth potential, but short-term gains could be modest, making timing and valuation key for new investors.

Is Micron a Good Buy?

Wall Street analysts maintain a Strong Buy rating on MU stock, with 26 Buys and two Holds assigned over the past three months. The recent rally has brought the stock close to Micron's average price target of $417.81, implying about 10% upside from current levels.
data.author: Kirti Tak
